title: DigiGait: Quantitative Gait Analysis in Mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7c8b","text":"This protocol describes the use of the DigiGait system (Mouse Specifics Inc.) for high-speed ventral-plane imaging and automated gait analysis in adult mice. The system quantifies multiple spatiotemporal gait parameters during treadmill locomotion.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9h0t3","text":"•       Use the same belt speed for all animals in a cohort.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"910qe","text":"•       Ensure the imaging surface is clean — debris causes tracking artifacts.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eme36","text":"•       Perform analysis blinded to genotype where possible.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"92lbd","text":"•       Start DigiGait system and DigiGait Analyzer software ≥15 min before use to allow warm-up.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ee58a","text":"•       Set treadmill belt speed appropriate for the study.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bq1n8","text":"•       Clean treadmill belt and transparent walking surface with 70% ethanol before each animal.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ee62","text":"•       Allow mice to acclimate to procedure room for ≥30 min before testing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2oqds","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"u6pj","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"ae7dt","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":""},"colTitles":["",""],"colWidths":[140,"376"],"data":[["\u003cb\u003eSubjects\u003c/b\u003e","Adult WT and KO littermates (~2 months old)"],["\u003cb\u003eApparatus\u003c/b\u003e","DigiGait imaging system (Mouse Specifics Inc., Framingham, Massachusetts, USA)"],["\u003cb\u003eCamera\u003c/b\u003e","High-speed ventral-plane camera (integrated)"],["\u003cb\u003eTreadmill belt speed\u003c/b\u003e","starting speed 10 cm/s, increasing to 15 cm/s once animal is walking steadily"],["\u003cb\u003eSoftware\u003c/b\u003e","DigiGait Analyzer software"],["\u003cb\u003eSession duration\u003c/b\u003e","~5–10 s of steady-state locomotion per animal"]],"guid":"0CA0C493B01F446A8402FFAD8342E104","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["\u003cb\u003eSubjects\u003c/b\u003e","Adult WT and KO littermates (~2 months old)"],["\u003cb\u003eApparatus\u003c/b\u003e","DigiGait imaging system (Mouse Specifics Inc., Framingham, Massachusetts, USA)"],["\u003cb\u003eCamera\u003c/b\u003e","High-speed ventral-plane camera (integrated)"],["\u003cb\u003eTreadmill belt speed\u003c/b\u003e","starting speed 10 cm/s, increasing to 15 cm/s once animal is walking steadily"],["\u003cb\u003eSoftware\u003c/b\u003e","DigiGait Analyzer software"],["\u003cb\u003eSession duration\u003c/b\u003e","~5–10 s of steady-state locomotion per animal"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23,23]}}}}
steps: Animal acclimation Gently place mouse on the stationary belt with the enclosure door closed. Allow 1–2 min for the animal to explore and calm before initiating belt movement.

Initiate belt movement Start the treadmill at 10 cm/s, then increasing to 15 cm/s once the animal is walking. Allow the mouse to reach steady-state locomotion (consistent running without stumbling or stopping). This typically takes 10–30 s.

Record gait Once steady-state locomotion is achieved, begin video capture for at least 5 full stride cycles (aim for 5–10 s of continuous recording). The ventral camera records paw placement for all four limbs simultaneously.
Return mouse to home cage Stop belt, open enclosure, and return mouse to its home cage. Clean belt surface with 70% ethanol before next animal.

Automated analysis Load video into DigiGait Analyzer. The software automatically segments individual paw areas across frames and computes gait parameters including: stride length, stride frequency, stance width (front/hind), paw area, swing time, stance time, and symmetry indices.
Data export and statistics Export gait parameters per animal. Compare WT vs KO groups using appropriate tests.
creator: Sarah van Veen
